name: Golden Pothos
description: An easy-care trailing vine with golden-variegated heart-shaped leaves. Perfect for beginners.
details: Golden Pothos is one of the most popular houseplants for a reason — it thrives on neglect and looks stunning doing it. The heart-shaped leaves trail beautifully from shelves and hanging baskets, growing several feet long over time. It purifies indoor air and adapts to almost any lighting condition, making it the ultimate starter plant.
care: Low to bright indirect light. Water when top inch of soil is dry. Tolerates low humidity but appreciates occasional misting. Fertilize monthly during spring and summer.

name: Zebra Plant White Wash
description: A striking tropical plant with bold white-veined leaves. A real eye-catcher on any shelf.
details: The Zebra Plant (Aphelandra squarrosa) earns its name from the dramatic white stripes on its deep green leaves. It's a showpiece houseplant that adds instant tropical flair to any room. When it's happy, it rewards you with bright yellow flower bracts that last for weeks. It likes a little more attention than your average houseplant, but the payoff is worth it.
care: Bright indirect light — no direct sun. Keep soil consistently moist but not soggy. Loves humidity — mist regularly or use a pebble tray. Feed every 2 weeks during spring and summer. Keep away from cold drafts.

name: Dieffenbachia
description: Bold tropical foliage with stunning green and yellow variegation. A lush statement plant.
details: Dieffenbachia (also called Dumb Cane) is a gorgeous tropical houseplant known for its large, paddle-shaped leaves splashed with green and yellow patterns. It fills out a space quickly and gives any room instant jungle vibes. It's relatively low-maintenance and does well in average household conditions. Just keep it away from curious pets — the sap can be irritating.
care: Medium to bright indirect light. Water when top inch of soil is dry. Avoid overwatering — yellowing lower leaves usually means too much water. Average household humidity is fine. Feed monthly in spring and summer.

name: Christmas Cactus
description: A festive blooming succulent with vivid pink flowers. Blooms reliably every holiday season.
details: Christmas Cactus (Schlumbergera) is a beloved houseplant that puts on a spectacular show of tubular pink, red, or white blooms right around the holidays. Unlike desert cacti, this one is a tropical forest plant that likes a bit more moisture and indirect light. With the right care, it can live for decades and gets passed down through generations. A true heirloom plant.
care: Bright indirect light — avoid direct sun. Keep soil lightly moist during growing season, slightly drier in fall to trigger blooming. Loves humidity. To encourage blooms, give it 12-14 hours of darkness and cooler temps (55-65°F) for 6 weeks in fall.

name: Spider Plant
description: A classic houseplant with arching green and white striped leaves. Nearly impossible to kill.
details: The Spider Plant (Chlorophytum comosum) is one of the most forgiving houseplants you can own. Its long, arching leaves with green and white stripes look great in hanging baskets or on shelves. As it matures, it sends out runners with baby plantlets (called spiderettes) that you can snip off and share with friends. It's also one of NASA's top air-purifying plants.
care: Bright to moderate indirect light. Water when top inch of soil is dry — they're drought tolerant and bounce back from underwatering. Average humidity is fine. Brown tips usually mean the water has too much fluoride — try filtered water. Feed monthly in spring and summer.

name: String of Pearls
description: A cascading succulent with bead-like leaves that spill beautifully from any planter.
details: String of Pearls (Senecio rowleyanus) is one of the most unique-looking succulents out there — its round, pea-shaped leaves trail down like a living beaded curtain. It's a head-turner in any hanging planter or on a high shelf. It's a succulent, so it stores water in those little pearls and doesn't need frequent watering. Give it bright light and let it dry out between waterings.
care: Bright indirect to direct light. Water sparingly — soak the soil and let it dry completely before watering again (every 1-2 weeks). Well-draining succulent soil is essential. Avoid getting water on the foliage. Fertilize once a month in spring and summer.

name: Tradescantia
description: A fast-growing trailing plant with richly colored leaves. Gorgeous in a hanging basket.
details: Tradescantia (sometimes called Wandering Dude or Inch Plant) is a fast-growing trailing houseplant loved for its richly colored foliage — deep greens, purples, and silvers depending on the variety. It fills out a hanging basket quickly and looks lush and full in no time. It's easy to propagate — just snip a stem, stick it in water, and you've got a new plant in days.
care: Bright indirect light brings out the best leaf color. Water when top inch of soil is dry. Pinch back leggy stems to keep it bushy and full. Average humidity is fine. Feed every 2 weeks during spring and summer. Propagates easily in water.

name: Norfolk Island Pine
description: A living Christmas tree that thrives indoors year-round. Elegant and evergreen.
details: Norfolk Island Pine (Araucaria heterophylla) is a graceful, symmetrical evergreen that makes a beautiful living Christmas tree — and unlike a cut tree, it keeps growing year after year. Its soft, feathery branches and tiered shape give it a naturally elegant look. It's a tropical plant despite the pine name, so it loves indoor life. Decorate it for the holidays or let it stand on its own as a statement plant.
care: Bright indirect light — some direct morning sun is fine. Keep soil evenly moist but not soggy. Loves humidity — mist regularly, especially in winter when indoor air is dry. Avoid cold drafts and heating vents. Feed monthly in spring and summer.
